frontmatter.name: adsense-readiness
frontmatter.description: Analyze websites and projects for Google AdSense compliance and readiness. Use when asked to check AdSense eligibility, audit a site for Google Ads, verify publisher policy compliance, prepare a site for monetization, or fix AdSense policy violations. Covers content quality, ad placement, privacy requirements, and technical standards.

usage_value.rationale: Niche but solid use case for content publishers and website owners seeking AdSense monetization.

security_risk.rationale: Pure prompt content with no scripts, URLs, or credential references.

best_practices.rationale: Has valid frontmatter and structured workflow, but lacks metadata block and triggers field.

evaluation.core_capabilities: Analyzes websites and projects for Google AdSense policy compliance, identifying issues that would block approval. Primary output: compliance report with specific remediation steps.

frontmatter.name: adsense-review
frontmatter.description: Perform a comprehensive live-site review mimicking the Google AdSense approval process. Use when a site has been rejected by AdSense (especially for "low value content"), when preparing to submit/resubmit a site for AdSense approval, or when diagnosing why a site keeps getting flagged. Requires a URL. Crawls the live site and evaluates it against every criterion Google reviewers check: content quality/depth/originality, navigation/UX, technical compliance, privacy policy, ads.txt, Better Ads Standards, and more.

usage_value.rationale: Niche but well-executed skill for publishers seeking live-site AdSense approval — specific audience, clear value.

security_risk.rationale: Pure prompt content with no scripts, external calls, or credential references.

best_practices.rationale: Has valid frontmatter and references, but lacks metadata block and triggers field.

evaluation.core_capabilities: Simulates the Google AdSense approval review process on live sites, identifying policy violations and content quality issues. Primary output: approval-readiness report with prioritized fixes.

frontmatter.name: agent-icon-creator
frontmatter.description: Generate AI agent icons, profile avatars, and logos as 1:1 ratio images using Google AI Studio Nano Banana model. Use when asked to "create an icon", "make an avatar", "generate a logo", "design an agent icon", "make a profile picture", or when the user needs any square visual identity asset for an AI agent, bot, app, or project. Also use when the user says "brand icon", "agent branding", "bot avatar", or wants to visually represent an agent concept.

metadata.triggers: agent icon, create icon, make avatar, generate logo, profile picture, agent branding, bot avatar, design icon, brand icon, visual identity

usage_value.rationale: Niche but solid — useful for teams building agent libraries who need branded visual assets.

security_risk.rationale: References Google AI Studio API key usage for image generation via Imagen 3.
findings: References API key credential for Google AI Studio image generation API

best_practices.rationale: Has full metadata block, references directory, and clear workflow with role definition.

evaluation.core_capabilities: Generates branded AI agent icons and avatars as 1:1 square images using Google AI Studio's Imagen 3 API, following visual design guidelines for agent profiles and documentation. Primary output: image generation prompts and workflow guidance.
external_requirements: Google AI Studio API (Imagen 3 for image generation)

frontmatter.name: agent-persona-creator
frontmatter.description: Create agent-agnostic AI personas using a four-layer architecture (Soul / Body / Faculty / Skill). Use when asked to create a persona, build a character, design an AI personality, make an agent persona, define a persona profile, or scaffold a persona bundle. Also use when someone wants to give an AI agent a persistent identity, backstory, or behavioral guide.

metadata.triggers: create persona, build character, design personality, agent persona, persona profile, AI identity, persona bundle, persona definition, persona json, four-layer persona

usage_value.rationale: Broadly useful — creating structured AI agent personas is a common need for teams building AI products and tools.

security_risk.rationale: No scripts or external calls; credential scanner false positive from discussion of API token concepts in persona content.

best_practices.rationale: Full metadata block, references directory, structured four-layer architecture workflow, and rich content.

evaluation.core_capabilities: Creates structured AI agent personas using a four-layer architecture (Soul/Body/Faculty/Behavior), producing platform-agnostic YAML persona documents for any AI assistant. Primary output: complete persona YAML with behavioral guidelines.

frontmatter.name: brainstorming
frontmatter.description: You MUST use this before any creative work - creating features, building components, adding functionality, or modifying behavior. Explores user intent, requirements and design before implementation.

usage_value.rationale: Niche — primarily useful as a companion skill within Claude Code / subagent workflows before creative or technical work.

security_risk.rationale: Pure prompt content with no scripts, URLs, or external dependencies.

evaluation.core_capabilities: Enforces a structured exploration phase before any creative or technical work, preventing premature implementation. Primary output: brainstorming session structure and decision checkpoint.

frontmatter.name: humanizer
frontmatter.description: Remove signs of AI-generated writing from text. Use when asked to humanize text, de-slop writing, make AI text sound human, rewrite AI-generated content, clean up AI writing, or detect AI patterns. Based on Wikipedia's "Signs of AI writing" guide. Detects and fixes 26 patterns including: inflated symbolism, promotional language, -ing phrases, AI vocabulary, sycophancy, filler, hedging, em dashes, rule of three, transition overuse, and chatbot artifacts.

usage_value.rationale: Broadly useful — rewriting AI-generated text to sound human is one of the most common content editing requests.

security_risk.rationale: Contains a single Wikipedia documentation URL as a content reference; no scripts or runtime network calls.

best_practices.rationale: Minimal frontmatter with no metadata block, no references directory, and limited structure.

evaluation.core_capabilities: Removes AI-writing patterns from text, rewriting content to eliminate overused phrases, hedging language, and formulaic structures while preserving meaning. Primary output: revised human-sounding text.

security_risk.rationale: Pure prompt content with no scripts or external dependencies.

best_practices.rationale: Minimal frontmatter at 317 tokens — no metadata block, no references directory.

evaluation.core_capabilities: Creates detailed Standard Operating Procedures written at a 5th-grade reading level through a clarifying question workflow, producing version-controlled step-by-step documentation. Primary output: structured SOP document with quality verification checklist.

frontmatter.name: startup-strategic-planning
frontmatter.description: Analyzes the founder's business context to deliver the 3 highest-impact next moves for growth (marketing or sales). Asks up to 10 diagnostic questions when needed to uncover bottlenecks, struggles, and opportunities. Use when user needs strategic guidance, next steps, growth planning, or actionable business strategy.

usage_value.rationale: Broadly useful — founders and early-stage teams regularly need actionable strategic direction tailored to their specific context.

security_risk.rationale: Pure prompt content with no scripts or external dependencies.

best_practices.rationale: No metadata block, no references directory, and no structured constraints section.

evaluation.core_capabilities: Analyzes founder business context to deliver 3 specific, high-impact growth moves with detailed playbooks, exact tactics, and immediate first actions tailored to the business stage and constraints. Primary output: 3-move strategic plan with execution playbooks.

frontmatter.name: strategy-research-analyst
frontmatter.description: Performs competitive analysis, technology trend assessment, signal classification, opportunity scoring, threat identification, and strategic intelligence briefings. Use when asked to research competitors, analyze market trends, assess technology signals, produce strategic briefings, evaluate threats and opportunities, perform industry analysis, score market opportunities, or deliver competitive intelligence.

metadata.triggers: competitive analysis, market research, technology trends, threat assessment, opportunity scoring, signal classification, strategic intelligence, competitor research, industry analysis, market sizing, SWOT

usage_value.rationale: Broadly useful — competitive intelligence and signal classification are needed by strategy teams, product managers, and executives.

security_risk.rationale: Pure prompt content with no scripts or external calls.

best_practices.rationale: Has metadata block with domain/role/scope, but very small body (166 tokens) — lacks references directory and constraints section.

evaluation.core_capabilities: Performs competitive intelligence analysis with signal classification, confidence scoring, and threat assessment, producing structured intelligence briefings from daily alerts to weekly strategic reports. Primary output: classified intelligence brief with confidence ratings.

frontmatter.name: tech-code-simplifier
frontmatter.description: Simplifies and refines code for clarity, consistency, and maintainability while preserving all functionality. Use when asked to simplify code, clean up code, refactor for readability, reduce complexity, apply coding standards, or improve code elegance. Focuses on recently modified code unless instructed otherwise.

usage_value.rationale: Broadly useful — code simplification and readability improvements benefit every development team.

security_risk.rationale: Pure prompt content with no scripts or external dependencies.

best_practices.rationale: Very minimal at 78 tokens — no metadata, no references, limited structure but clear scope.

evaluation.core_capabilities: Simplifies recently modified code for clarity and maintainability without changing behavior, applying project-specific conventions and eliminating code smells and anti-patterns. Primary output: refactored code with improved readability.

frontmatter.name: tech-finishing-a-development-branch
frontmatter.description: Use when implementation is complete, all tests pass, and you need to decide how to integrate the work - guides completion of development work by presenting structured options for merge, PR, or cleanup

usage_value.rationale: Broadly useful for development workflows — structured branch completion with verified options prevents common mistakes.

security_risk.rationale: Pure prompt content; git and gh CLI are invoked through the agent's existing terminal access, not additional external dependencies.

best_practices.rationale: Clear structured workflow with explicit options, constraints, and a quick reference table, but lacks metadata block.

evaluation.core_capabilities: Guides completion of development work by verifying tests pass, presenting 4 structured integration options (merge, PR, keep, discard), and executing the chosen workflow including git worktree cleanup. Primary output: integrated and cleaned development branch.

frontmatter.name: tech-prd-generator
frontmatter.description: Generates professional PRD (Product Requirements Document) files optimized for AI coding tools. Takes a rough product idea, asks clarifying questions, and outputs a structured PDF ready to feed into AI coding assistants.

usage_value.rationale: Broadly useful — generating structured, AI-optimized PRDs from rough ideas saves significant product planning time.

security_risk.rationale: Instructions include running 'npx md-to-pdf' which installs and executes an npm package for PDF conversion.
findings: Instructions invoke 'npx md-to-pdf' to install and run an npm package for PDF conversion

best_practices.rationale: Has references directory with PRD template and structured clarifying question workflow, but lacks metadata block.

evaluation.core_capabilities: Transforms rough product ideas into comprehensive AI-ready PRDs through a clarifying question workflow, saving output as both Markdown and PDF. Primary output: structured PRD document with acceptance criteria, data models, and AI implementation notes.

frontmatter.name: tech-receiving-code-review
frontmatter.description: Use when receiving code review feedback, before implementing suggestions, especially if feedback seems unclear or technically questionable - requires technical rigor and verification, not performative agreement or blind implementation

usage_value.rationale: Broadly useful for development workflows — structured, technically rigorous code review reception prevents implementation errors.

security_risk.rationale: Pure prompt content with no scripts or external dependencies.

best_practices.rationale: Clear structured workflow with forbidden patterns, source-specific handling, and concrete examples, but lacks metadata block.

evaluation.core_capabilities: Enforces technically rigorous code review responses — verifying before implementing, structured pushback reasoning, and factual acknowledgment without performative agreement. Primary output: evaluated and prioritized implementation plan responding to feedback.

frontmatter.name: tech-requesting-code-review
frontmatter.description: Use when completing tasks, implementing major features, or before merging to verify work meets requirements

usage_value.rationale: Broadly useful for developer workflow — structured code review dispatch as a regular practice improves code quality systematically.

security_risk.rationale: Pure prompt content; subagent dispatch is a platform capability, not an external dependency.

best_practices.rationale: Clear workflow with integration guidance and example patterns, but lacks metadata block.

evaluation.core_capabilities: Dispatches a code-reviewer subagent with a structured review template after completing development tasks, providing severity-triaged feedback and an action protocol. Primary output: code review initiation with tracked follow-up actions.

frontmatter.name: tech-test-driven-development
frontmatter.description: Use when implementing any feature or bugfix, before writing implementation code

usage_value.rationale: Essential — TDD discipline is foundational software engineering practice that prevents a broad class of defects and improves design.

security_risk.rationale: Pure prompt content with no scripts or external dependencies.

best_practices.rationale: Detailed Red-Green-Refactor constraints with concrete good/bad examples, but lacks metadata block and references directory.

evaluation.core_capabilities: Enforces strict Red-Green-Refactor TDD discipline, requiring a failing test before any implementation code, with explicit verification steps for each phase. Primary output: test-first implementations with verified Red-Green-Refactor cycle completion.

frontmatter.name: tech-writing-plans
frontmatter.description: Use when you have a spec or requirements for a multi-step task, before touching code

usage_value.rationale: Broadly useful for developer workflows — structured implementation plans prevent scope creep and missed context during multi-step development.

security_risk.rationale: Pure prompt content with no scripts or external dependencies.

best_practices.rationale: Minimal at 150 tokens — no metadata, no references, and limited structural scaffolding.

evaluation.core_capabilities: Creates comprehensive bite-sized implementation plans for multi-step development tasks with exact file paths, code samples, test commands, and expected outputs for every step. Primary output: structured implementation plan document saved to docs/plans/.

frontmatter.name: ux-design-personas
frontmatter.description: Create UX/UI design artifacts from research through specs. Use when asked to build personas, journey maps, information architecture, content structure, navigation, user flows, UX/UI specs, screen mockups, layout rules, field rules, or interaction behaviors.

usage_value.rationale: Broadly useful — UX artifacts from personas to screen specs are needed throughout product development by designers and product teams.

security_risk.rationale: Pure prompt content with no scripts or external dependencies.

best_practices.rationale: No metadata block, no references directory, and limited structural scaffolding despite covering a broad scope.

evaluation.core_capabilities: Creates UX/UI design artifacts including personas, journey maps, information architecture, content structure, navigation flows, and screen layout specs. Primary output: complete set of UX documentation artifacts from research through production-ready specs.
